title: Hoverboard
explanation:
This is an interactive comic made to celebrate the release of Randall's new book, Thing Explainer, which was released on the same day as this comic. It is a platforming minigame, whose ostensible goal is collecting gold coins. This interactive comic appeared on the same date as the release of Randall's book, Thing Explainer. 

The title refers to the skateboard-like  that was prominently featured in the movie . The BTTF movies' hero, , travels to the future in this sequel, to 2015-10-21, to be exact. This comic was released on 2015-11-24, about a month later. Hoverboards are just one of many things predicted in Back to the Future Part II that had actually come to pass by this date, along with inventions like flying cars and musical clothing. Randall is known to have had electric skateboards, which which are thematically related, and another type of hoverboard would be mentioned just five weeks later in 1623: 2016 Conversation Guide.

Apart from the date of release and the text about the book, there are several scenes in the game that could be seen as references to Thing Explainer. Instead, the main part of the game is all the references to several movies and other stuff that has often been featured in xkcd as well as many of the other comics.



===Game features===
Your player in the game is an  Cueball riding a hoverboard that assumes several different positions as it travels. You control your player with the left, up and right arrows, as noted on the start screen. Your player can jump repeatedly and thereby reach ever-increasing heights. You can move him left and right both while jumping and descending. (More under Functionality and Controls.)

Your player begins in a maze (called the Play Area). It holds 17 gold coins and contains a "deposit" box. One goal of the game is to collect as many coins as possible and deposit them quickly. Deposits returns text messages describing the result of the round of play. It may seem to some, at this point, that there is nothing more to the game.

However, just out of view, beyond the maze on either side are tall walls seemingly designed to contain the player. But  these walls can be scaled by continuous jumping, allowing the player to exit the maze on both sides, despite the flashing red false warning error message: "Return to the Play Area".

If you disregard this, you can take your player, hoverboard Cueball, to explore far beyond the walls to left and right (and above), where there are many points of interest to be found, as well as 152 more coins to collect, 116 on the right and 36 on the left.

All of the interesting places of the game are noted in the references and maps below and on the Extra Hoverboard Pages that are linked there. As a practical matter, however, you can only visit many of the most interesting places only with the help of the JavaScript Goggles hack. Entering Goggles Mode brings up a new pane to the right of the comic that enables you to see secret entrances in otherwise solid surfaces, and to see in the dark after the player enters black spaces.

To open the JavaScript Console, press F12 on PCs, and Command-Option-i on Mac. In the command line under the Console tab, enter: ze.goggles ( ) , making sure your command includes spaces before, between and after the two parens. This will bring up a window to the right of the comic showing black objects that are right next to the player in red. The red marks the spots where the player can pass objects that are black in the game. Cyan lines in the window mark the limits of how far the player can pass in the dark.

Hoverboard's many  recall comic 1110: Click and Drag. Nonetheless, Hoverboard is xkcd's first actual game, one in which you move an object in front of the drawing. In Click and Drag you only move the drawing into the view section. In the two previous April Fools' Day comics, 1350: Lorenz and 1506: xkcloud, the user does not move anything, but only supplies choices, text and drawings. The next  comic contains another game, 1663: Garden, in which you also moved objects around and make them disappear (as you do with coins in this game.)

===Main themes===
There are many themes and references throughout the game, but the two main ones are  and . Below is a list of details and references to these movies (19 to SW and 6 to LOTR) as well as much more.

The largest part of the game is located on the right side of the play area and is dominated by references to Star Wars, notably a recreation of the opening scene in the , where  space ship , also known as The Rebel Blockade Runner, is flying over the desert planet . Here it is followed by the , Devastator. The desert ground with these two space ships above is the first you find to the right. Although in the game they are located inside the atmosphere, not as in the movie out in space, a fact that is commented on by Megan who looks out from an opening in the bottom of the Destroyer's hull. 
:Megan: Is this ship designed to fly in the atmosphere like this? 

Both ships are high above the ground, but there are three wires from the ground that connect (and thus guide you) up to The Runner and from there 100+ torpedoes, which are fired down on the Runner coming from the Destroyer above, gives away the location of the Destroyer itself. The Destroyer is so high up in the air, that you would not be likely to find it by chance without these leads (or at least some of the several other hints from people on the ground looking up and reacting or like White Hat looking from the tip of the Washington Monument and reacting by saying: "Uh, what the heck is that?!."). There is also a giant island floating very high above the flagpost in the left side of the world, with no indication of its existence.

One of the main points (The Joke) of the entire game is the extreme size of such a Destroyer. It takes up more space than the rest of the game (and most of the coins are located there). Inside the Destroyer there are many, many rooms, long corridors and deep shafts, even a huge cave with trees and animals. At the bridge we meet both  and the Emperor himself, . There are many other jokes related both to Star Wars and other movies and xkcd comics inside the Destroyer. For instance, Darth Vader discusses , bringing up Rose Quartz and Steven himself (who can be found with the Crystal Gems in a different part of the Destroyer).
:Darth Vader: But Steven's mother is a Crystal Gem...
There are also interesting things and references along the ground beneath the Destroyer, some of these are also Star Wars related like the wedding scene where  is mentioned. 

To the left side is an entirely different world, although much smaller (shorter) than the one to the right. Here, the main theme is The Lord of the Rings mainly with the presence of a volcano where for instance Megan tries to throw in , as in the scene from the  where  fails and  attacks inside the volcano , thus making it clear that the volcano in the xkcd game represents Mt. Doom. Frodo, , and  are all mentioned in the game. The first two by name in the text. Two  are talking about Frodo and , Sauron's name is read up by Megan from a book, and Gandalf is drawn as a wizard figure in the crater and also his name is used in the message you receive if you collect all possible coins.  

The two worlds do conflict though, as an  is parked at a gas station to the left in LOTR world, and Gandalf's famous quote You shall not pass! from his fight against the  is modified by Cueball who is not trying to block your path to a coin inside a room in the Destroyer. This conflict could very well be a reference to the fact that  who played  in LOTR, now plays the  in the new Star Wars movie, and his appearance as Snoke is not that much different from Gollum's. Snoke also delivers a line in the movie (about "bringing someone to me") that reminds one of Gollum when he talks about "the ring coming to me". Randall would not have known this last part at the time that this comic was released, but he would likely be aware of Andy Serkis' involvement.

Other themes are related to other movies, like a naughty reference to  from inside the bridge of the Rebel Blockade Runner where a long-haired woman (maybe Danish since she obviously does this to annoy Star Wars fans and Hairbun behind her, which could represent Princess Leia with her special hair style) delivers the following line taken from the Star Trek universe: 
:Long haired woman: Captain's log, stardate November 24th, 2015...
:Hairbun: Augh! No!

Also references to video games are seen, best shown with the maze towards the back end of the Destroyer which are an almost complete depiction of Level 1 of the computer game  down to the three coins being in the place of three items to take in that game, and at least two of the opposing sword fighters in the correct places as well. 

The comic/game and book were released only 3 weeks before the 7th Star Wars movie  was released on December 14, 2015. And this movie had already been referenced by Randall 7½ weeks earlier in 1585: Similarities, when another movie () were released. It was a reference in the title text to the new robot BB-8 that are featured in the Force Awakens. That the Star Wars section is thus so much larger than the LOTR section could be a hint to the fact that it is now 12 years ago that the last of those films were released, and although  has been released as  over the previous three Christmases it was Star Wars that dominated the Christmas a month after this comic's release.

===Extra Hoverboard pages===
Since this comic is so big and complicated extra pages have been created to include much more information than is wished for on this main page (which is already of considerable size). These pages are listed here for convenience, but they are also listed in the relevant sections below:

*1608: Hoverboard/Transcript - The full transcript of the entire comic, as if you played the game can be found here. It is linked from the Transcript section.

*1608: Hoverboard/The whole image - A collection of different compilations of the whole Image. It is linked from the Environment section.

*1608: Hoverboard/Images of secret passages - Images with an overview of the secret passages in the game. It is linked from the Secret passages and hidden places section.

*1608: Hoverboard/All image links It is linked from the All images section.

*1608: Hoverboard/Images coins It is linked from the Coins section.

*1608: Hoverboard/Screen-shots It can be linked from several sections, but among others from the Coins section.

*1608: Hoverboard/Images of individual scenes It will be linked from the list of details and references, which will be using these images in the explanation.

===Characters===
Almost all main characters in the xkcd gallery are used more than once, especially there are three characters that are used several times. Number one is of course Cueball (with close to 90 appearances) who is already shown at the starting point. He is also the first character seen when walking both right and left (where he speaks the first line to the left).

Then follows Megan with at least 40 appearances, she is even shown twice in the same location three times, one of these three even occurs within one of the small images. She is also shown with Cueball in his first appearance to the left, and she is the first to speak a line to the right here. 

Also Ponytail is well represented with 26 appearances (for instance here). 

Hairy (with different hair styles) is used 7 times (for instance here) and Hairbun is used 5 times (for instance here). 

Two of the main characters are used twice: Beret Guy (here and here) and White Hat (here and here).

The only exception is that the main villain of xkcd Black Hat who is very hard to find. He is only shown once in the comic where he very uncharacteristically just sits and snores at the right wall in the cave inside the Destroyer (is it really him...?). The only other hint that he is not forgotten is that his hat can be seen on a stick under the Destroyer after the three large T-like structures.  And this is not that kind of stick characters Randall usually draws. But at least we now know where Danish has hidden it from him this time (see 405: Journal 3). Given that his hat is stuck there, he could also be any of the Cueballs close by…

Also Blondie seems to be only represented once, while in her reporter mode while discovering a bug before the volcano as can be seen here. 

There are also several characters that are not recognizable as either any of the standard xkcd characters or in any other way (see for instance a few of the wedding guests). But there are possibly some of the minor characters are used like Danish at the bridge in the Blockade Runner (here) but this cannot be confirmed, she just looks like her and has some similarities teasing the Star Wars fan.

Several places in the comic it is clear from the size and the behavior that some of the characters are kids, see for instance the Blondie image mentioned above, where two kids looking like Megan and Hairy stand behind her, but they would in principle not be Megan or Hairy despite appearance, as these named characters are adults.

There are also many fictive people from different movies, books, and shows; for instance Darth Vader (here), Gandalf (here) and Steven Universe (here) all three mentioned above, and some real life people: you can find Elon Musk, who has a hidden lair under the Volcano (see here), and Gregor Mendel, hanging out on a Star Destroyer with a pea plant.

Furthermore, there are many animals (like the puma and the deer) in the cave, some even rather big like this octopus or even gigantic like this ant queen (all three from inside the Destroyer). There are also a couple of small Star Wars robots, one in a corridor in each spaceship (here from the Destroyer).
